chapter.title: Data Types and Model Item Properties (XForms 1.1 Chapters 5 and 6)
chapter.para: This chapter documents data typing support (including XSD-derived constraints) and the mapping between XForms model item properties and runtime state propagation into rendered controls.

section.title: Data types and XSD support
section.para: SaxonForms provides partial XSD type validation through the xsd-helpers.xsl package (~148 lines). Type validation occurs during the revalidate phase via check-instance-xsi-types() (saxon-xforms.xsl:1706).

section.title: Type normalization
section.para: The xsdh:canonical-type() function (xsd-helpers.xsl:16) normalizes type QNames to a canonical form. It handles the xs: and xsd: prefixes and maps type names to their XSD 1.1 canonical equivalents. This normalization ensures consistent type comparison regardless of prefix usage in the XForms source.

section.title: Type validation
section.para: The xsdh:is-type-valid() function (xsd-helpers.xsl:41) validates a string value against an XSD type name. It supports the common XSD simple types: xs:string, xs:integer, xs:decimal, xs:double, xs:float, xs:boolean, xs:date, xs:dateTime, xs:time, xs:anyURI, and others. Validation uses castable as expressions where possible.

Limitation: The type MIP on xforms:bind is parsed but not fully enforced — the bind @type attribute is recognized but instance data is not rejected based on type mismatch during the revalidate phase. This affects 6 W3C tests related to schema type validation.

section.title: Type coercion in XPath evaluation
section.para: XPath 3.1 is stricter about type coercion than XForms expects. SaxonForms wraps calculate and relevant expressions in try/catch blocks to handle empty-string arithmetic crashes (e.g., '' + 1 throws in XPath 3.1 but should produce NaN in XForms). This was resolved for tests 6.1.5.a and 6.1.4.b.

Similarly, the xforms:min() and xforms:max() wrappers (xforms-function-library.xsl:270/288) return NaN instead of throwing XPath 3.1 type errors when applied to non-numeric sequences, matching XForms semantics (resolved: 7.7.2.b, 7.7.3.b).

section.title: Model item properties to runtime/DOM mapping
section.para: MIPs are defined on xforms:bind elements within xforms:model. During the rebuild phase, binds are processed by add-context mode (L5321) to resolve their @nodeset to absolute instance paths. During recalculate, xforms-recalculate-binding (L5431) evaluates each MIP expression.

section.title: readonly
section.para: The @readonly MIP expression is evaluated as a boolean. When true(), SaxonForms sets the HTML disabled/readonly attribute on the corresponding control. Readonly propagates by inheritance: if a parent bind is readonly, child binds inherit readonly status. Resolved: 6.1.2.a, 6.1.2.b (readonly MIP enforcement now propagates to HTML inputs, including inheritance from ancestor bindings).

section.title: relevant
section.para: The @relevant MIP expression is evaluated as a boolean. When false(), the bound control is hidden from the DOM. The refreshRelevantFields-JS template (L4550) and getRelevantStatus template (L4244) handle the show/hide logic during the refresh phase. Controls within an irrelevant group or case are also hidden.

section.title: required
section.para: The @required MIP expression is evaluated as a boolean. When true(), the control is marked with a CSS class (via getHtmlClass, L4175) and empty values cause the node to be invalid. Validation is performed by check-required-fields() (L1576) and check-required-bindings() (L1681).

section.title: constraint
section.para: The @constraint MIP is an XPath boolean expression evaluated against the bound node. When the constraint evaluates to false(), the node is invalid and xforms-invalid is dispatched. Validation is performed by check-constraints-on-fields() (L1626) and check-constraints-on-bindings() (L1727). Constraint validation events (xforms-valid/xforms-invalid) now dispatch after setvalue changes constraint state (resolved: 6.1.6.a).

section.title: calculate
section.para: The @calculate MIP is an XPath expression whose result replaces the bound node's value on each recalculate cycle. Evaluation is handled by xforms-recalculate-binding (L5431) using evaluate-xpath-with-context-node() (L1448). Calculate expressions containing position() or last() have actual nodeset position/size pre-substituted before evaluation (resolved: 7.2.e).

section.title: Binding resolution
section.para: When a control references a bind (via @bind) or a nodeset (via @ref/@nodeset), the get-properties mode template (L637) resolves the binding to a concrete instance node and matching bind element. The resolution process:

listitem.para: If @bind is present, look up the bind element by ID ($binding-referenced-by-id, L664).
Resolve the model context ($context-model, L687) — either from the bind's parent model or the default model.
Resolve the instance context ($instance-context, L740) — extracted from the nodeset expression via xforms:getInstanceId() (L4140).
If no explicit bind, match by nodeset: evaluate both the control's @ref and each bind's @nodeset against the instance, and compare resulting nodes ($binding-matching-nodeset, L754).

section.title: Data types and MIP propagation diagram
section.para: The following sequence diagram illustrates the flow from bind definition through rebuild, recalculate, revalidate, and refresh, showing how MIP values propagate to DOM controls.

listitem.para: Targeted W3C coverage: tests/w3c/ch05.spec.ts (15 tests, 73% pass) and tests/w3c/ch06.spec.ts (11 tests, 82% pass).
Cross-check chapter behavior statements against known resolved/gap entries: readonly enforcement (6.1.2.a/b), type coercion (6.1.5.a, 6.1.4.b), constraint events (6.1.6.a).
Verify schema type validation limitation is accurately described per STATUS.md.
Run full regression (npm run test:gate) before chapter sign-off.
